impact: Moves the two repeatedly blocked validator tasks out of selectable checkbox form, preserves completed progress, and appends deterministic recovery tasks that let the daemon resume autonomous fixture-first work without retrying malformed Python or validating a known-missing fixture path.
summary: Replan blocked PP&D lifecycle recovery tasks into narrower fixture-first and syntax-first daemon work.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 2 task(s) are blocked: Task checkbox-27: Recovery for checkbox-27: add the one-file mutation validator for `ppd/tests/fixtures/lifecycle/permit_lifecycle_requirement_extraction_expected_output.json` using only syntactically simple Python, standard-library `unittest`, exact fixture loading, an initial committed-fixture-valid assertion, one mutation per test, and an explicit `py_compile`-first self-check. Cover missing `categoryId`, missing `sourceEvidenceIds`, missing local `evidence[].evidenceId`, out-of-range `confidence`, duplicate `(categoryId, action, object, sourceEvidenceIds)` tuples, and malformed stop-gate classification. Do not add extractor implementation code.; Task checkbox-29: Add a one-file exact-path validator for `ppd/tests/fixtures/lifecycle/permit_lifecycle_process_index.json` that checks each referenced skeleton fixture exists, each category id is unique, each entry cites only committed lifecycle fixture paths, and no entry authorizes live crawling, login, payment, submission, upload, certification, cancellation, official refund, official extension, or inspection scheduling.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
